beats.narration_text: Namaste — this is Liam, in for Bear. Ask Claude to build a complex dashboard, a multi-tab tool, or anything requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components as a claude.ai artifact. The Web Artifacts Builder fires. It is not for simple single-file HTML. It provisions a full React 18 plus TypeScript plus Tailwind plus shadcn/ui stack in one init script, then bundles everything down to a single self-contained HTML file you can share directly. It also has one explicit design mandate: no AI slop — centered layouts, purple gradients, uniform rounded corners, and Inter font are named as patterns to avoid. Let me show you the pipeline, the stack, and where the mandate lives.

props.command: Build me a project tracker dashboard with kanban board, filtering, and progress charts as a shareable claude.ai artifact.

beats.narration_text: The skill is a four-step pipeline. Step one: initialize with bash scripts/init-artifact.sh, passing a project name. That single command creates a fully configured project — React 18 with TypeScript via Vite, Tailwind CSS 3.4.1 with the shadcn/ui theming system, path aliases at the at-slash prefix, more than forty shadcn/ui components pre-installed with all Radix UI dependencies, and Parcel configured for bundling. One command, zero manual setup. Step two: develop by editing the generated code. Step three: bundle with bash scripts/bundle-artifact.sh. That script installs Parcel and html-inline, builds the project, and inlines all JavaScript, CSS, and dependencies into a single self-contained HTML file called bundle.html. Step four: share bundle.html in the conversation and display it to the user. Testing is listed as step five but is explicitly optional — do not test upfront, it adds latency. Test only after sharing the artifact, if issues arise or the user requests it.

props.sparkLine: Init once. Develop. Bundle. Share bundle.html.

beats.narration_text: The design section is one of the shortest in any Anthropic skill, but the most direct: avoid what is referred to as AI slop. Four specific patterns are named. Excessive centered layouts. Purple gradients. Uniform rounded corners. Inter font. That is the entire rule — negative, explicit, and actionable. No positive design system is provided, which means you need taste. What the bundle provides: React and ReactDOM, Tailwind utility classes and shadcn/ui component styles, all Radix UI primitives for accessibility, and any other dependencies your code imports. The output is a single HTML file. The requirement for bundling is that your project must have an index.html in the root directory. If that is missing, bundle-artifact.sh fails. Node 18 or higher is required — the init script auto-detects and pins the Vite version accordingly.

props.sparkLine: No centered layouts. No purple gradients. No Inter font.

beats.act: teardown — what it gets right / where it bites
beats.narration_text: Here is the teardown. The architectural insight of the Web Artifacts Builder is that it eliminates the React project bootstrapping problem and the bundling problem simultaneously: one init script provisions the full stack, and one bundle script collapses it into a single shareable HTML file. What it gets right: init-artifact.sh provisions React, TypeScript, Tailwind, 40-plus shadcn/ui components, and Parcel in a single command — zero manual setup; the bundle step produces a single self-contained HTML artifact; testing is explicitly deferred to avoid latency; and the anti-AI-slop rule names the four worst patterns directly. Where it bites: Node 18 and npm must be pre-installed; the bundle step with Parcel can fail on complex dependency graphs with no debugging guidance in the skill; the anti-slop rule is entirely negative — no design token system or positive recipe replaces the named bad patterns; the description says the skill supports routing but there is no react-router setup in the init script; and there is only one reference link, the shadcn/ui component docs.

props.sparkLine: One init. One bundle. One shareable HTML file.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. The Web Artifacts Builder is for complex claude.ai artifacts — state management, routing, shadcn/ui components — not simple HTML. One init script: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind 3.4.1, 40+ shadcn/ui components, Parcel. One bundle script: inlines all JS, CSS, and deps into bundle.html. Testing is optional and explicitly deferred. Design mandate: avoid centered layouts, purple gradients, uniform rounded corners, and Inter font. The rule is negative only — taste required. Node 18+ and an index.html in root are required for bundling to succeed.

artifactLines: For: complex artifacts (state mgmt / routing / shadcn/ui) — NOT simple HTML
bundle-artifact.sh: Parcel build → html-inline → single self-contained bundle.html
Testing: explicitly optional and deferred — build first, test only on request
Design: NO centered layouts · NO purple gradients · NO uniform corners · NO Inter
Requirements: Node 18+, index.html in root; anti-slop rule is negative only — taste required

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Open a Claude Code session. Paste this: Build me a note-taking app with tags, search, and a markdown editor as a shareable claude.ai artifact. Use the Web Artifacts Builder. Then watch four things: first, does Claude run init-artifact.sh before writing any component code? Second, does it use shadcn/ui components from the pre-installed set rather than writing its own UI from scratch? Third, does it run bundle-artifact.sh and share bundle.html at the end — not a raw index.html or a dev server URL? And fourth, does the visual output avoid the named AI slop patterns: no dominant centered layout, no purple gradient, no every-element-same-radius corners, no Inter font? If Claude skips the init script and writes React without the scaffold, the bundle step will not work. If it shares an index.html instead of bundle.html, the artifact is not self-contained. Those are your gates.

props.command: Build a note-taking app with tags, search, and a markdown editor. Shareable claude.ai artifact. Use the Web Artifacts Builder.
props.runningText: Watch: init-artifact.sh run first? shadcn/ui components used? bundle.html shared (not index.html)?
output: Expected: init-artifact.sh runs before any component code
Red flag: no anti-slop check, or raw index.html shared instead of bundle.html

beats.narration_text: Web Artifacts Builder. Liam, in for Bear.
